evidence: Paper explicitly states: "We estimated variable importance using a permutation approach and the root mean square error (RMSE) as primary performance evaluation metric." This is not impurity-based importance. To validate findings, authors state: "To reinforce validity of the findings from variable importance in the random forest models, we conducted a principal component analysis (PCA) from the training set data and calculated loadings" and confirm "Results from PCA loadings were in agreement with these findings." Authors discuss ranking: "Ranking the importance of ROH parameters helped us to better understand these distinguishing characteristics" and note context-dependent importance across different prediction tasks.
